minutes_briefs.title: BC private training rules updated for health occupation definitions
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has changed the definition of 'clinical placement' and 'preceptor' in the Private Training Regulation. Instead of referring to 'a career occupation in the health field', the rules now use the term 'health occupation'. A 'health occupation' is defined as any career in Category 3 of the National Occupational Classification (a standard list of job types used across Canada). This change took effect on April 1, 2026. It affects how private training schools must classify their programs and placements.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review your school's program descriptions and clinical placement agreements to ensure they use the new 'health occupation' definition.

key_dates.label: BC Private Training Regulation definition change takes effect

minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 45/2026
Made under: Private Training Act
Published in: No. 6 – B.C. Regs. 43 to 56, April 7, 2026
Deposit date: March 30, 2026

B.C. Reg. 45/2026, deposited March 30, 2026, under thePRIVATE TRAINING ACT[section 63]. Ministerial Order M148/2026, dated March 4, 2026. I, Jessie Sunner, Minister of Post-Secondary Education and Future Skills, order that, effective April 1, 2026, section 15 of the Private Training Regulation, B.C. Reg. 153/2016, is amended (a)	in paragraph (a) of the definition of"clinical placement"by striking out "in a career occupation in the health field" and substituting "in a health occupation", (b)	by adding the following definition: "health occupation"means a career occupation that is described in Category 3 of the current edition of the National Occupational Classification;, and (c)	in the definition of"preceptor"by striking out "in the health field" and substituting "in a health occupation". — J. SUNNER,Minister of Post-Secondary Education and Future Skills.
